summary: PR #2567 split the former item translation unit src/melee/it/it_266F.c into itspawn, itgroundcoll, itdraw, and itdrop, adding corresponding headers and updating GALE01 splits, symbols, and configure.py object entries. The automated report showed net matched-code and linked-code gains, with old it_266F matches appearing as broken because the symbols moved into the new units.

what_changed: Deleted src/melee/it/it_266F.c and introduced four split item TUs: itspawn.c, itgroundcoll.c, itdraw.c, and itdrop.c.
Added new headers for the split units and reduced the old it_266F.h header.

Updated configure.py so the former it_266F object became four object entries; only itgroundcoll.c is shown as Matching in the loaded diff, while itspawn.c, itdraw.c, and itdrop.c are shown as NonMatching.
Updated GALE01 split ranges: itspawn covers text 0x8026C47C-0x8026D564, itgroundcoll 0x8026D564-0x8026EB18, itdraw 0x8026EB18-0x8026F3AC, and itdrop 0x8026F3AC-0x8026F9A0.
Adjusted adjacent item TU section ownership in splits.txt for itcoll, itmaplib, it_279C, and itzako rodata/text boundaries.
Updated symbols.txt with a local rodata label at 0x803B85A8 and changed two sdata2 symbols at 0x804DC6A8 and 0x804DC6AC from global it_* names to local @304/@305 symbols.
Automated report recorded +0.07% matched code, +2700 matched-code bytes, +0.14% linked code, and 65 new matches; old it_266F entries appeared as broken matches because the unit was removed.

smart_moves.text: The split was coordinated across source files, headers, configure.py, splits.txt, and symbols.txt rather than only moving C code.
evidence_refs: changed_files list includes source, headers, config/GALE01/splits.txt, config/GALE01/symbols.txt, configure.py

smart_moves.text: The PR preserved or re-established section boundaries for adjacent item TUs after removing it_266F, including explicit rodata ownership for itcoll, itmaplib, it_279C, and itzako.
evidence_refs: splits diff: itcoll, itmaplib, it_279C, itzako rodata changes

smart_moves.text: The configure.py status split made per-TU matching state visible: itgroundcoll was configured as Matching while the other new units remained NonMatching.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a large decomp TU, update the object list and section split metadata together, including adjacent TU boundaries that inherit text or rodata ranges.

Automated match reports for TU splits may show many broken matches in the deleted old unit alongside new matches in the replacement units; interpret the old-unit breakage as relocation evidence rather than a simple regression when the PR is explicitly a split.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment: 65 new matches and 54 broken matches in old it_266F

naming_conventions.convention: The new item TU names describe functional slices of the former address-style it_266F unit: itspawn, itgroundcoll, itdraw, and itdrop.
Unproven data symbols may remain address-style or local-label style after split metadata changes; this PR changed some sdata2 globals to local @304/@305 labels and added a local ...rodata.0 label.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: TU splitting by contiguous text ranges: the former it_266F text range was divided into four named TUs with explicit start/end addresses in splits.txt.
Move or reassign rodata/sdata2 section ownership when the split boundary exposes data belonging to adjacent or newly split units.
evidence_refs: splits.txt diff: rodata and sdata2 ranges
symbols.txt diff: local rodata and sdata2 label changes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: No new low-level matching tactic such as PAD_STACK, pragma steering, register use, or inline asm can be confidently attributed to this PR from the loaded evidence; such constructs appear in the deleted old-file excerpt but the loaded new-file diffs are truncated.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt is truncated after old it_266F deletion
deleted old-file excerpt includes PAD_STACK and #pragma dont_inline

No human review comments or review records were present in the loaded PR slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: TU splits require coordinated config, split, symbol, and header updates
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2567 is evidence that splitting a large item TU involved deleting the old TU, adding new named TUs and headers, replacing the configure.py object entry, and adjusting split and symbol metadata including adjacent section ownership.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Deleted-unit broken matches can be expected in a TU split report
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2567, the automated report lists new matches in itspawn/itgroundcoll/itdraw/itdrop and broken matches in deleted it_266F; for split PRs, the old-unit broken rows should be interpreted alongside the new-unit rows and net metrics.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all four new TUs were matching; the loaded configure.py diff marks only itgroundcoll.c as Matching.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are present.

Do not promote PAD_STACK or pragma usage as a successful tactic for this PR from loaded evidence because the visible occurrences are in the deleted old-file excerpt and the new-file diffs are truncated.
Do not treat the symbols.txt local-label changes as a global naming rule without broader evidence.
